opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My right side feels like it's being continuously electrocuted and burned with a hot iron. I thought I was having a heart attack until these blisters showed up today.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Three days of severe right-sided chest burning pain, now with a blistering band-like rash.

symptom_narrative.onset: severe burning/electric pain 72h ago, rash today
symptom_narrative.character: continuous burning, electric, stabbing pain in a band on one side; now blisters
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right chest, in a band from the back to the front, stopping at the midline
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: tingling/hypersensitivity of the skin; malaise, low fever; no true shortness of breath or exertional pattern

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: had chickenpox as a child; otherwise generally well

communication_style.typical_message_style: in severe pain, feared a heart attack

objective_data_offered.value: a band of red skin with clusters of clear blisters on the right chest, from the back around to the front, stopping exactly at the midline; only one side
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: camera view; cannot auscultate or do an ECG

objective_data_offered.value: heart rate ~95, blood pressure 148/92, temperature 37.6; breathing not truly short but shallow due to pain
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report; cardiac/respiratory status cannot be verified remotely
